danger_signals.visual_form: Competing watercraft visible on isometric playfield, distinct from player speedboat
danger_signals.meaning: Active threats that attack player and endanger swimmers; must be destroyed or avoided

danger_signals.visual_form: Human figures positioned near water edges or being dragged toward lake perimeter
danger_signals.meaning: Rescue objectives under threat; failure to retrieve endangered swimmers results in life loss

danger_signals.visual_form: Bomb objects scattered across waterscape requiring collection and transport
danger_signals.meaning: Hazardous payload requiring careful navigation to detonation sites; timing and positioning critical

danger_signals.visual_form: Shoreline boundaries and mid-river obstructions (logs, whirlpools) in racing levels
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental collision hazards that cause navigation failure and time loss
Temporal danger state; failure to complete objectives before timer expires causes life loss

safety_signals.meaning: Upgrades to weapon capability and boat speed; enhance combat effectiveness and survivability

safety_signals.visual_form: Clear navigable pathways in isometric view, absence of obstacles
safety_signals.meaning: Safe movement corridors; allow rapid transit and evasion
Tactical advantage enabling airborne movement for shortcuts and obstacle avoidance

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Cannon-equipped speedboat distinguished from competitor boats in races
Visually distinct watercraft without player's weaponry; engaged in head-to-head racing
Hostile watercraft actively attacking swimmers and endangering rescue objectives
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Behavior pattern: dragging swimmers toward lake edges

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multiple boats visible on screen competing for position; timer displayed; obstacles and riverbanks emphasized
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player boat must outpace timer and competitors while navigating hazards; combat with other racers available

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Swimmers depicted at water surface or being dragged by rogue boats; rescue zones marked
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must intercept rogue boats, prevent swimmer abduction, manually return captured swimmers to center water

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Four bomb objects positioned on playfield; detonation site designated
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must collect bombs with protection mechanism and transport to specific location; careful navigation required to prevent detonation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Large or distinct enemy entity replacing standard race/objective format at level conclusion
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Escalated combat requirements; player must defeat boss to advance

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dynamic obstacles (logs, whirlpools) positioned throughout waterway; increased environmental hazard density
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player evasion-focused gameplay; combat reduced; obstacle avoidance primary mechanic

environmental_storytelling.example: Transition from racing to rescue to bomb defusal across 25 graduated difficulty levels communicates escalating mission complexity and world danger intensity
Logs, whirlpools, and rogue boats positioned in water environment directly indicate environmental hostility and ongoing threats requiring active response

3D isometric camera angle provides omniscient view of mission space, allowing player to perceive spatial relationships between threats (competitor boats, swimmers, obstacles) and communicate environmental topology
Player speedboat explicitly depicted with cannon armament; distinguishes player capability and faction identity from civilian/rescue roles

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Pursues optimal racing line; engages competitors with cannon fire; maintains speed awareness of timer
Intercepts rogue boats; prevents swimmer abduction; performs manual rescue of captured swimmers
Navigate toward finish; avoid obstacles; attempt to outpace player; can be disabled via cannon fire
Target swimmers; drag victims toward lake perimeter; must be destroyed or intercepted before swimmer capture completes
Stationary or dragged by rogue boats; require active rescue intervention; halfway capture point triggers mandatory manual return action
Presents escalated combat challenge; behavior pattern suggests distinct AI/attack patterns differentiated from standard enemy boats

notes: Cobra Triangle employs graduated difficulty progression across 25 levels to communicate evolving world threats. Danger signals are predominantly behavioral (enemy actions) and temporal (timer pressure) rather than static visual indicators. The isometric perspective enables simultaneous threat visualization—allowing players to perceive spatial relationships between multiple danger sources (competitor boats, obstacles, swimmers under threat). World-state transitions between racing/rescue/defusal are communicated through objective-specific spawning and goal positioning rather than dramatic visual environmental changes. The game lacks faction-based political complexity; conflict is mechanically-driven (competition, rescue, destruction) rather than narrative-driven. Power-ups provide clear positive feedback signals that enhance player capability and safety perception.
